messages.content: You are a Lean 4 autoformalization agent working in a current Mathlib-only project. The target Lean statement, target declaration name, and original proof are intentionally withheld. Avoid stale Lean 3/old Mathlib syntax and identifiers; follow the lean_environment/current_version_guidance and local Lean examples. Return exactly one JSON object.

    "Do not use sorry, admit, aesop? placeholders, or comments standing in for proof.",

    "Do not assume access to the withheld target Lean statement or name.",
    "For multi-part TeX chunks, formalize only the specified labeled part/source span. Do not conjoin all parts unless the record explicitly asks for the whole multi-part result.",
    "Do not cite or invent raw helper names that are not present in the Lean prefix context/local examples; prefer displayed local style and standard Mathlib APIs.",
    "Do not redeclare definitions, structures, abbrevs, notation helpers, or instances already present in the Lean prefix context; reference them directly.",
    "Do not introduce theorem-local `where` definitions or redefine concepts such as summability, limits, binomial coefficients, or matrix operations. If a needed definition is not in context, state a narrower theorem using the displayed APIs.",
    "Use current Lean 4/Mathlib syntax and API names; if your memory conflicts with displayed local context, trust the displayed context.",
    "State a single proposition-level theorem/lemma that is likely to match the specified source part; do not bundle typeclass instances or unrelated source parts into conjunctions.",
    "Every nonstandard helper theorem or local API used in the proof should appear explicitly in the Lean prefix context or local examples. If it is not displayed, do not use its name.",
    "If an existing theorem or lemma in the prefix context has binders, apply it to the needed variables rather than using the theorem constant bare.",
    "Prefer the narrowest theorem directly supported by the source sentence; avoid generalizing to a stronger forall/if statement unless that exact shape is in the source or prefix context.",
    "Prefer a short proof if the prefix context already contains the needed fact."
  ],
  "context": {
    "source_statement_or_chunk": [
      {
        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/FPS/DividingFPS.tex",
        "line_range": [
          422,
          460
        ],
        "original_line_range": [
          423,
          453
        ],
        "line_range_adjustment_reasons": [
          "expanded_backward_to_include_environment_begin",
          "expanded_forward_to_close_environment"
        ],
        "labels": [
          "thm.binom.upneg-n"
        ],
        "snippet": "\\begin{theorem}\n\\label{thm.binom.upneg-n}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{C}$ and $k\\in\\mathbb{Z}$. Then,%\n\\[\n\\dbinom{-n}{k}=\\left(  -1\\right)  ^{k}\\dbinom{k+n-1}{k}.\n\\]\n\n\\end{theorem}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Theorem \\ref{thm.binom.upneg-n} (sketched).]If $k<0$, then this is\ntrivial because both $\\dbinom{-n}{k}$ and $\\dbinom{k+n-1}{k}$ are $0$ (by\n(\\ref{eq.def.binom.binom.eq})). Thus, we WLOG assume that $k\\geq0$. Hence,\n$k\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Thus, (\\ref{eq.def.binom.binom.eq}) yields%\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dbinom{-n}{k}  &  =\\dfrac{\\left(  -n\\right)  \\left(  -n-1\\right)  \\left(\n-n-2\\right)  \\cdots\\left(  -n-k+1\\right)  }{k!}\\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\ \\text{and}%\n\\\\\n\\dbinom{k+n-1}{k}  &  =\\dfrac{\\left(  k+n-1\\right)  \\left(  k+n-2\\right)\n\\left(  k+n-3\\right)  \\cdots\\left(  k+n-k\\right)  }{k!}.\n\\end{align*}\nA moment of thought reveals that the right hand sides of these two equalities\nare equal up to a factor of $\\left(  -1\\right)  ^{k}$. Thus, so are the left\nhand sides. In other words, $\\dbinom{-n}{k}=\\left(  -1\\right)  ^{k}%\n\\dbinom{k+n-1}{k}$. This proves Theorem \\ref{thm.binom.upneg-n}.\n\\end{proof}\n\n(Quick exercise: Rederive Example \\ref{exa.binom.-1choosek} from Theorem\n\\ref{thm.binom.upneg-n}.)\n\nNext, we show a formula for negative powers of $1+x$:\n\n\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.fps.anti-newton-binom}For each $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$, we have%\n\\[\n\\left(  1+x\\right)  ^{-n}=\\sum_{k\\in\\mathbb{N}}\\left(  -1\\right)  ^{k}%\n\\dbinom{n+k-1}{k}x^{k}.\n\\]\n\n\\end{proposition}\n"
      }
    ],

        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean",

        ],
        "text": "*Theorem (thm.binom.upneg-n)**: Upper negation formula for binomial coefficients.\nFor `r` in a BinomialRing `R` and `k ∈ ℕ`, we have `C(-r, k) = (-1)^k * C(r+k-1, k)`.\n\nNote: In Mathlib, generalized binomial coefficients are defined via `Ring.choose`\nfor `BinomialRing`s. This generalizes the classical formula for `n ∈ ℂ`.\nLabel: thm.binom.upneg-n",
        "policy": "source-facing Lean doc comment only; target Lean declaration name and statement remain withheld"
      },
      "target_comment_labels_or_parts": [
        "thm.binom.upneg-n"
      ],
      "context_mode": "target-comment",
      "instruction": "If the source snippet contains multiple labeled or numbered parts, formalize only the specific part/source span indicated by specific_source_labels, specific_labeled_parts, or target_declaration_source_comment. Do not conjoin all parts unless the record labels identify the whole multi-part result. The target_declaration_source_comment is source-facing prose only; it is not the hidden Lean target statement."
    },

    "lean_prefix_context": "-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:41-41 (open)\nopen scoped BigOperators\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:43-43 (namespace)\nnamespace AlgebraicCombinatorics\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:51-51 (variable)\nvariable {K : Type*} [CommRing K]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:195-197 (fps_invertible_iff_constantCoeff)\ntheorem fps_invertible_iff_constantCoeff (a : PowerSeries K) :\n    IsUnit a ↔ IsUnit (a.constantCoeff) :=\n  PowerSeries.isUnit_iff_constantCoeff\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:215-217 (fps_inv_coeff_zero)\ntheorem fps_inv_coeff_zero {F : Type*} [Field F] (f : PowerSeries F) :\n    PowerSeries.coeff 0 f⁻¹ = (PowerSeries.coeff 0 f)⁻¹ := by\n  simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_zero_eq_constantCoeff, PowerSeries.constantCoeff_inv]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:281-294 (fps_isUnit_inv_eq_inv)\nlemma fps_isUnit_inv_eq_inv {F : Type*} [Field F] (f : PowerSeries F) (hf : IsUnit f) :\n    (↑hf.unit⁻¹ : PowerSeries F) = f⁻¹ := by\n  have hfeq : (↑hf.unit : PowerSeries F) = f := IsUnit.unit_spec hf\n  have hne : PowerSeries.constantCoeff f ≠ 0 := by\n    rw [PowerSeries.isUnit_iff_constantCoeff] at hf\n    exact isUnit_iff_ne_zero.mp hf\n  have hmul : f * ↑hf.unit⁻¹ = 1 := by\n    calc f * ↑hf.unit⁻¹ = ↑hf.unit * ↑hf.unit⁻¹ := by rw [hfeq]\n         _ = ↑(hf.unit * hf.unit⁻¹) := by rfl\n         _ = ↑(1 : (PowerSeries F)ˣ) := by rw [mul_inv_cancel]\n         _ = 1 := by rfl\n  symm\n  rw [PowerSeries.inv_eq_iff_mul_eq_one hne, mul_comm]\n  exact hmul\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:299-301 (fps_inv_coeff_zero_isUnit)\ntheorem fps_inv_coeff_zero_isUnit {F : Type*} [Field F] (f : PowerSeries F) (hf : IsUnit f) :\n    PowerSeries.coeff 0 (↑hf.unit⁻¹ : PowerSeries F) = (PowerSeries.coeff 0 f)⁻¹ := by\n  rw [fps_isUnit_inv_eq_inv f hf, fps_inv_coeff_zero]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:306-311 (fps_inv_coeff_succ_isUnit)\ntheorem fps_inv_coeff_succ_isUnit {F : Type*} [Field F] (f : PowerSeries F) (hf : IsUnit f) (n : ℕ) :\n    PowerSeries.coeff (n + 1) (↑hf.unit⁻¹ : PowerSeries F) =\n      -(PowerSeries.coeff 0 f)⁻¹ *\n        ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), PowerSeries.coeff (k + 1) f *\n          PowerSeries.coeff (n - k) (↑hf.unit⁻¹ : PowerSeries F) := by\n  simp only [fps_isUnit_inv_eq_inv f hf, fps_inv_coeff_succ]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:321-323 (fps_onePlusX_isUnit)\ntheorem fps_onePlusX_isUnit : IsUnit (1 + PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries K) := by\n  rw [fps_invertible_iff_constantCoeff]\n  simp [PowerSeries.constantCoeff_X]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/DividingFPS.lean:328-361 (fps_onePlusX_inv)\ntheorem fps_onePlusX_inv {F : Type*} [Field F] :\n    (1 + PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)⁻¹ = PowerSeries.mk fun n => (-1 : F) ^ n := by\n  -- (1 - X)⁻¹ = mk 1 (the power series with all coefficients 1)\n  have h1 : (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)⁻¹ = PowerSeries.mk 1 := by\n    have hc : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) ≠ 0 := by simp\n    symm\n    rw [PowerSeries.eq_inv_iff_mul_eq_one hc]\n    exact PowerSeries.mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one F\n  -- rescale (-1) maps (1 - X) to (1 + X)\n  have h2 : PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) = 1 + PowerSeries.X := by\n    ext n\n    simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_rescale, map_sub, map_one, PowerSeries.coeff_one]\n    cases n with\n    | zero => simp\n    | succ n =>\n      simp only [Nat.succ_ne_zero, ↓reduceIte, PowerSeries.coeff_X]\n      split_ifs with h\n      · simp [h]\n      · have hn : n ≠ 0 := by omega\n        simp [PowerSeries.coeff_X, hn]\n  -- rescale preserves inverses (as a ring homomorphism)\n  have h3 : PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) ((1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)⁻¹) =\n      (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X))⁻¹ := by\n    have hc : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) ≠ 0 := by simp\n    have hc' : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X)) ≠ 0 := by\n      rw [h2]; simp\n    symm\n    rw [PowerSeries.inv_eq_iff_mul_eq_one hc', ← (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F)).map_mul,\n        PowerSeries.inv_mul_cancel _ hc]\n    simp\n  -- Combine: (1+X)⁻¹ = rescale(-1)((1-X)⁻¹) = rescale(-1)(mk 1) = mk ((-1)^n)\n  rw [← h2, ← h3, h1]\n  ext n\n  simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_rescale, PowerSeries.coeff_mk, Pi.one_apply, mul_one]",
    "lean_environment": {
      "toolchain": "leanprover/lean4:v4.28.0",
      "imports": [
        "Mathlib"
      ],
      "current_version_guidance": [
        "Generated code is checked with the repository lean-toolchain and current Mathlib, not Lean 3 or an older Lean 4 snapshot.",
        "Prefer Lean 4 syntax: `fun x => ...`, `by` tactic blocks, namespaces/dot notation as shown in the prompt, and current Mathlib names from displayed context.",
        "Do not invent old/deprecated identifiers such as guessed `*_apply`, `det_swap_rows`, `CommAlgebra`, or `LaurentPolynomial.X` unless they are explicitly present in context; use displayed local APIs or prove by unfolding/simping.",
        "For power-series coefficients, follow the local examples' argument order such as `coeff n (X : R⟦X⟧)`, not `coeff (X : R⟦X⟧) n`.",
        "For Laurent polynomial samples, prefer displayed current APIs such as `LaurentPolynomial.T n` and local notation `K[T;T⁻¹]`; do not use bare polynomial `X` for Laurent polynomials.",
        "Do not make typeclass objects (`CommRing α`, `Algebra R A`, etc.) components of an `∧`; those are types/classes, not propositions. Put them as assumptions/instances or use theorem statements about terms instead.",
        "If the source theorem is a narrow identity, formalize that identity directly rather than a broad bundled theorem whose components will not match the grader's withheld statement."
      ],
      "available_reference_corpus": [
        "Current-version mathlib source can be searched locally at /tmp/mathlib4-v4.28.0-src when preparing/evaluating prompts.",
        "Use nearby repository Lean prefix/local examples first; use mathlib source snippets only as API/style evidence, not as a source for the withheld target statement."
      ]
    },
    "local_lean_style": {
      "guidance": [
        "Match the local Lean style shown in local_lean_style.examples when it applies; prefer exact APIs and argument order already used in this file.",
        "Use only identifiers that appear in the Lean prefix context/local examples or are standard Mathlib identifiers; do not cite or invent raw helper names from guessed notation expansions.",
        "If the local examples use a domain-specific API such as `Ring.choose`, `updateCol`, or a local theorem name, keep that exact API family instead of falling back to a more familiar but different API such as `Nat.choose` or `Matrix.updateColumn`."
      ],
      "notation_contracts": [],
      "examples": [
        "theorem fps_onePlusX_inv {F : Type*} [Field F] :\n    (1 + PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)⁻¹ = PowerSeries.mk fun n => (-1 : F) ^ n := by\n  -- (1 - X)⁻¹ = mk 1 (the power series with all coefficients 1)\n  have h1 : (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)⁻¹ = PowerSeries.mk 1 := by\n    have hc : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) ≠ 0 := by simp\n    symm\n    rw [PowerSeries.eq_inv_iff_mul_eq_one hc]\n    exact PowerSeries.mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one F\n  -- rescale (-1) maps (1 - X) to (1 + X)\n  have h2 : PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) = 1 + PowerSeries.X := by\n    ext n\n    simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_rescale, map_sub, map_one, PowerSeries.coeff_one]\n    cases n with\n    | zero => simp\n    | succ n =>\n      simp only [Nat.succ_ne_zero, ↓reduceIte, PowerSeries.coeff_X]\n      split_ifs with h\n      · simp [h]\n      · have hn : n ≠ 0 := by omega\n        simp [PowerSeries.coeff_X, hn]\n  -- rescale preserves inverses (as a ring homomorphism)\n  have h3 : PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) ((1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F)⁻¹) =\n      (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X))⁻¹ := by\n    have hc : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (1 - PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries F) ≠ 0 := by simp\n    have hc' : PowerSeries.constantCoeff (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F) (1 - PowerSeries.X)) ≠ 0 := by\n      rw [h2]; simp\n    symm\n    rw [PowerSeries.inv_eq_iff_mul_eq_one hc', ← (PowerSeries.rescale (-1 : F)).map_mul,\n        PowerSeries.inv_mul_cancel _ hc]\n    simp\n  -- Combine: (1+X)⁻¹ = rescale(-1)((1-X)⁻¹) = rescale(-1)(mk 1) = mk ((-1)^n)\n  rw [← h2, ← h3, h1]\n  ext n\n  simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_rescale, PowerSeries.coeff_mk, Pi.one_apply, mul_one]",
        "theorem fps_onePlusX_isUnit : IsUnit (1 + PowerSeries.X : PowerSeries K) := by\n  rw [fps_invertible_iff_constantCoeff]\n  simp [PowerSeries.constantCoeff_X]",
        "theorem fps_inv_coeff_succ_isUnit {F : Type*} [Field F] (f : PowerSeries F) (hf : IsUnit f) (n : ℕ) :\n    PowerSeries.coeff (n + 1) (↑hf.unit⁻¹ : PowerSeries F) =\n      -(PowerSeries.coeff 0 f)⁻¹ *\n        ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), PowerSeries.coeff (k + 1) f *\n          PowerSeries.coeff (n - k) (↑hf.unit⁻¹ : PowerSeries F) := by\n  simp only [fps_isUnit_inv_eq_inv f hf, fps_inv_coeff_succ]",
        "theorem fps_inv_coeff_zero_isUnit {F : Type*} [Field F] (f : PowerSeries F) (hf : IsUnit f) :\n    PowerSeries.coeff 0 (↑hf.unit⁻¹ : PowerSeries F) = (PowerSeries.coeff 0 f)⁻¹ := by\n  rw [fps_isUnit_inv_eq_inv f hf, fps_inv_coeff_zero]"
      ]
    },
    "domain_statement_shape_guidance": [
      {
        "domain": "negative binomial formal power series",
        "trigger": "source context describes inverse powers of `1 + X` and negative binomial coefficients",
        "preferred_statement_family": [
          "For the negative-binomial inverse-power theorem, use the exact local binder/typeclass shape `{F : Type*} [Field F] [BinomialRing F] (n : ℕ)`.",
          "State the right hand side as `PowerSeries.mk fun k => (Ring.choose (-(n : ℤ)) k : F)`; keep the negative upper argument in `ℤ` and cast the coefficient to `F`.",
          "Prefer the displayed local helper `fps_onePlusX_pow_neg' n` when it appears in context; if Lean needs explicit type arguments, write them by name, e.g. `fps_onePlusX_pow_neg' (F := F) n`."
        ],
        "avoid_statement_family": [
          "Do not change the upper argument to `-(n : F)` or leave `BinomialRing`/coefficient types to metavariable inference.",
          "Do not replace the local `Ring.choose` family with `Nat.choose` or an ad hoc binomial coefficient definition.",
          "Do not apply the helper as `fps_onePlusX_pow_neg' F n`; the first explicit positional argument is the natural exponent, not the type."
        ]
      }
    ],
    "mathlib_context": [

    ],
